editableNewDialogue.text: If I don’t have a measuring spoon, how can I roughly measure a tablespoon?

A tablespoon is about 15 mL. If you have kitchen items: 3 teaspoons = 1 tablespoon, and a standard bottle-cap-sized measure is often close to 1 teaspoon (varies). You can also use a liquid measuring cup: 1 tablespoon = 1/16 cup.
What if I only have a regular spoon from the drawer?

Regular eating spoons vary a lot, so it’s approximate. A “tablespoon” measuring spoon is usually a bit smaller than a big soup spoon and bigger than a small dessert spoon. If you’re eyeballing powder, level it off with a knife or the edge of a cup to be more consistent.
Does the type of cocoa powder change how much I should use?

Yes. Unsweetened cocoa is more intense and bitter, so you might use less (and add sweetener). Hot chocolate mix is already sweetened and lighter in cocoa flavor, so you might use more. Also, some powders clump—stirring into a small splash of warm milk first can help.
Random question: is it true that some countries measure tablespoons differently?

Yes—there are slight differences. In the US, 1 tablespoon is ~14.8 mL; in many places it’s rounded to 15 mL; and in Australia a tablespoon is 20 mL. Recipes usually work fine with small differences, but it matters more for baking.
I only really lift after school, so I’m trying to figure out if the chocolate milk fits better right when I get home or once I’m done.

If your workouts are after school, chocolate milk usually makes more sense after you finish lifting, since it helps with recovery (carbs + protein). If you drink it right when you get home, just leave enough time so it doesn’t feel heavy during the workout. If you want, tell me roughly how long after getting home you start lifting and how long you train.
About the chocolate milk thing—I tried two spoonfuls, but it was way too strong for me. I’ve been doing just one spoonful lately.
Got it—if two spoonfuls tastes too strong, one spoonful makes sense. You can always adjust upward if you want it richer, but it sounds like one is your sweet spot.
